{{日本の氏族
|家名 = Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko
|家紋 = <!-- 家紋の画像 -->
|家紋名称 =
|本姓 = {{ill|Nagasa clan|ja|長狭氏|lt=Possibly Nagasa clan|lt_ja=長狭氏か}}
|家祖 = {{ill|Takenuta-no-Mikoto|ja|武沼田命}}
|種別 = {{ill|Kōbetsu|ja|皇別}}
|出身地 = Yamato Province
|根拠地 = Nagasa Province (later {{ill|Nagasa District|ja|長狭郡}} and {{ill|Asai District|ja|朝夷郡}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|ja|安房国|lt=Awa Province|lt_ja=安房国}})
|人物 =
|支流 =
}}
'''Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' (''nagasa no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''nagasa kokuzō'') was a {{ill|kuni no miyatsuko|ja|国造}} who ruled the region that later became the eastern part of {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|ja|安房国|lt=Awa Province|lt_ja=安房国}} ('''[[#Territory|Nagasa Province]]''').

The "{{ill|Kokuzō Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" (volume 10 of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}'') records the establishment dates and appointed figures of all kuni no miyatsuko nationwide, but there is no entry for Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko; the existence of this kuni no miyatsuko is confirmed from the {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} chapter of the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}''.

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}'' it is written '''Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''.

=== Ancestry ===
*According to the chapter on {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} (the first {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}}) in the ''Kojiki'', '''{{ill|Kan'yaimimi-no-Mikoto|ja|神八井耳命}}''', imperial {{ill|prince|ja|皇子}} of that emperor and elder brother of {{ill|Emperor Suizei|ja|綏靖天皇}} (the 2nd emperor), was the ancestor of the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko and others. Of the same lineage as {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}, {{ill|Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造}}, and others.

=== Clan ===
The clan of the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko is unknown, but descendants of the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko include the Nagasa clan (see [[#Descendants]]), so the clan may have been '''{{ill|Nagasa clan|ja|長狭氏}}''' (''nagasa-uji''). The {{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} is also unknown, but many kuni no miyatsuko nationwide held the kabane of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}} (see [[List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|国造#諸国造一覧]]), so the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko may also have held the Atai kabane. From the contents of the ''Kojiki'' and the genealogies discussed [[#Ancestry|above]], it can be seen that they shared an ancestry with the {{ill|Ofu clan|ja|意富氏}} ({{ill|kabane|ja|カバネ|lt=kabane|lt_ja=姓}} of {{ill|Omi (kabane)|ja|臣|lt=Omi|lt_ja=臣}}), the {{ill|Tsuge clan|ja|都祁氏}}<ref group="注">The clan of the {{ill|Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|闘鶏国造}}.</ref> (kabane of {{ill|Atai (kabane)|ja|直 (姓)|lt=Atai|lt_ja=直}}), the {{ill|Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|石城国造|lt=Michinoku Iwaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt_ja=道奥石城国造}}, the {{ill|Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|仲国造|lt=Hitachi Naka no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt_ja=常道仲国造}}, and others.

== Base ==
The base of the kuni no miyatsuko was the later '''{{ill|Nagasa District|ja|長狭郡}}''' of {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|ja|安房国|lt=Awa Province|lt_ja=安房国}}<ref>''{{ill|Nihon Rekishi Chizu|ja|日本歴史地図|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chizu: Primitive and Ancient Volume, Vol. 2|lt_ja=日本歴史地図　原始・古代編　下}}''.</ref>, corresponding roughly to most of present-day {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|ja|鴨川市|lt=Kamogawa City|lt_ja=鴨川市}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. The '''{{ill|Hiroba Kofun Cluster|ja|広場古墳群}}''' in {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|ja|鴨川市|lt=Kamogawa City|lt_ja=鴨川市}} has been identified as the base of the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko (see [[#Tombs]])<ref name="日本辞典">[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263220.html Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko (Awa)] - Nihon Jiten (accessed February 6, 2018 5:38 PM {{ill|Japan Standard Time|ja|日本標準時|lt=JST|lt_ja=日本標準時}})</ref>.

The municipality that bore the name "Nagasa" was {{ill|Nagasa, Chiba|ja|長狭町|lt=Nagasa Town|lt_ja=長狭町}}.

=== Territory ===
The territory of the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko was the region then called '''Nagasa Province'''. Nagasa Province corresponded to the eastern part of the later {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|令制国}} of {{ill|Awa Province (Chiba)|ja|安房国|lt=Awa Province|lt_ja=安房国}} ({{ill|Nagasa District|ja|長狭郡}} (the {{ill|Kamo River (Chiba)|ja|加茂川 (千葉県)|lt=Kamo River|lt_ja=加茂川}} basin) and {{ill|Asai District|ja|朝夷郡}} (the {{ill|Maruyama River|ja|丸山川}} basin)), equivalent to parts of present-day {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|ja|鴨川市}} and {{ill|Minamibōsō, Chiba|ja|南房総市}} in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}.

The fact that the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko is not listed in the "{{ill|Kokuzō Hongi|ja|国造本紀}}" (volume 10 of the ''{{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}''), which records the establishment dates and appointed figures of all kuni no miyatsuko nationwide, is thought to be because it lost its power by the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}, bordered to the north by {{ill|Iji Miyake|ja|伊甚屯倉}} (mentioned in the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}'' in the 4th month of the first year of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} ([[534]])), and pressured from the south by the {{ill|Awa no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|阿波国造}}.

Eleven provinces including Nagasa ({{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国造|lt=Awa Province|lt_ja=阿波国}}, Nagasa, {{ill|Sue Province|ja|須恵国造|lt=Sue Province|lt_ja=須恵国}}, {{ill|Mukuda Province|ja|馬来田国造|lt=Mukuda Province|lt_ja=馬来田国}}, {{ill|Kikuma Province|ja|菊麻国造|lt=Kikuma Province|lt_ja=菊麻国}}, {{ill|Iji Province|ja|伊甚国造|lt=Iji Province|lt_ja=伊甚国}}, {{ill|Kami-tsu-Unakami Province|ja|上海上国造|lt=Kami-tsu-Unakami Province|lt_ja=上海上国}}, {{ill|Muza Province|ja|武社国造|lt=Muza Province|lt_ja=武社国}}, {{ill|Shimo-tsu-Unakami Province|ja|下海上国造|lt=Shimo-tsu-Unakami Province|lt_ja=下海上国}}, {{ill|Chiba Province|ja|千葉国造|lt=Chiba Province|lt_ja=千葉国}}, and {{ill|Imba Province|ja|印波国造|lt=Imba Province|lt_ja=印波国}}) were called {{ill|Fusa Province|ja|総国|lt=Fusa Province (Fusa Province)|lt_ja=総国（捄国）}}. According to the ''{{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}'', because the hemp planted by Ame-no-Tomi-no-Mikoto grew well, the land was named "Fusa Province" (one theory: "Fusa Circuit") from "''fusa''," an alternative name for hemp. In the first year of {{ill|Emperor Ankan|ja|安閑天皇}} ([[534]]) (according to the ''{{ill|Teiō Hennenki|ja|帝王編年記}}''), 8 of the provinces of Fusa Province, including Nagasa (Awa, Nagasa, Sue, Mukuda, Kikuma, Iji, Kami-tsu-Unakami, Muza), separated to become {{ill|Kazusa Province|ja|上総国|lt=Kazusa Province (Kami-tsu-Fusa Province)|lt_ja=上総国（上捄国）}}. There is also a view that this separation occurred in the mid-{{ill|6th century|ja|6世紀}}, based on the "Kami-" (upper) prefix in a manner similar to Kōzuke Province, which was separated from {{ill|Kenu|ja|毛野|lt=Kenu Province|lt_ja=毛野国}}<ref>Kusuhara Yūsuke et al. (eds.), ''Kodai Chimei Gogen Jiten'' "Fusa" entry, {{ill|Tōkyōdō Shuppan|ja|東京堂出版}}, 1981. ISBN 4-490-10148-1</ref>. Kazusa Province became a {{ill|ritsuryō province|ja|令制国}} in the {{ill|7th century|ja|7世紀}}. On {{ill|Yōrō|ja|養老}} 2, 5th month, 2nd day ({{ill|June 4, 718|ja|5月2日 (旧暦)|lt=June 4, 718|lt_ja=718年6月4日}}), the 4 districts of {{ill|Hei District|ja|平郡|lt=Heguri District|lt_ja=平群郡}}, {{ill|Awa District|ja|安房郡}} (the two above being from former {{ill|Awa Province|ja|阿波国造|lt=Awa Province|lt_ja=阿波国}}), {{ill|Asai District|ja|朝夷郡}}, and {{ill|Nagasa District|ja|長狭郡}} (the two above being from former Nagasa Province) were separated from Kazusa Province to newly establish Awa Province; but on {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 13, 12th month, 10th day ({{ill|January 20, 742|ja|12月10日 (旧暦)|lt=January 20, 742|lt_ja=742年1月20日}}), Awa Province was once again merged into Kazusa Province. Thereafter, in the first year of {{ill|Tenpyō-hōji|ja|天平宝字}} ([[757]]), Awa Province was re-established with the original 4 districts.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
The ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'' does not list any {{ill|Engishiki-listed shrines|ja|式内社}} in Nagasa District, and it is unknown what the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko had as a {{ill|tutelary kami|ja|氏神}} or what kind of {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} they enshrined.

=== Tombs ===
* '''{{ill|Hiroba Kofun Cluster|ja|広場古墳群}}'''
*: A {{ill|kofun cluster|ja|古墳群}} located in {{ill|Kamogawa, Chiba|ja|鴨川市|lt=Kamogawa City|lt_ja=鴨川市}} (formerly Nagasa District) in {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|ja|千葉県}}. It is considered to be the tomb of the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko<ref name="日本辞典"/>. In the later {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, a hollowed-out {{ill|boat-shaped sarcophagus|ja|舟形石棺}} made of {{ill|sandstone|ja|砂岩}} was discovered in one of the {{ill|round kofun|ja|円墳}} in this cluster<ref name="南房総の歴史">[https://www.mboso-etoko.jp/kyoudo/gyousei/kodai_01.html History of Minamibōsō: Administrative History] (accessed February 6, 2018 6:27 PM {{ill|Japan Standard Time|ja|日本標準時|lt=JST|lt_ja=日本標準時}})</ref>. The Kamogawa example is the only excavation of a hollowed-out boat-shaped sarcophagus in the prefecture<ref name="南房総の歴史"/>.

== Descendants ==
* '''{{ill|Nagasa clan|ja|長狭氏}}'''
*: A clan based in {{ill|Nagasa District|ja|長狭郡}} (see [[#Territory]] above)<ref name="日本辞典"/>. The ''{{ill|Seishi Kakei Daijiten|ja|姓氏家系大辞典. 第3巻|lt=Seishi Kakei Daijiten, Vol. 3|lt_ja=姓氏家系大辞典. 第3巻}}'' states, "They were likely descendants of the Nagasa no Kuni no Miyatsuko."

== Footnotes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
=== Notes ===
{{Reflist|group="注"}}
=== Citations ===
{{Reflist|2}}

== References ==
* Ogasawara Nagakazu (supervisor), ''Chiba-ken no Chimei (Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 12)'', Heibonsha, 1996, ISBN 4-582-49012-3, p. 1063
* {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=太田亮|authorlink=太田亮|url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/1131019/47|accessdate=2018-02-06|year=1936|chapter=|title=姓氏家系大辞典. 第3巻|publisher=[[姓氏家系大辞典刊行会]]|isbn=|page=4163|ref=seishi}}Link is to the {{ill|National Diet Library Digital Collections|ja|国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション}}, frame 47.
* {{Cite book|和書|author=|editor=[[竹内理三]]等|title=[[日本歴史地図]]　原始・古代編　下|year=1982|publisher=[[柏書房]]|isbn=|page=289|chapter=|ref=chizu}}

== See also ==
* [[List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|国造#諸国造一覧]]

== External links ==
* [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/nagasa.html Walking the Kofun of Bōsō: Kofun of Nagasa Province]

{{DEFAULTSORT:なかさのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:安房国]]
[[Category:千葉県の歴史]]
[[Category:多氏|#なかさのくにのみやつこ]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:長狭国造]]
[[Category:Need translation]]
